results.taskDescription: Fetch top 15 HN posts and write a paragraph-style summary categorizing them by topic. Tests substantive synthesis from observation data.
results.output:
# Today on Hacker News

The discussions on Hacker News today were heavily dominated by the rapid evolution and ethical considerations surrounding Artificial Intelligence, alongside deep dives into low-level programming and hardware development. In the AI sphere, the conversation was highly technical, ranging from philosophical discussions on how "AI should elevate your thinking, not replace it," to practical advancements like the "Show HN: AI memory with biological decay (52% recall)." The industry itself was grappling with its own metrics, highlighted by the report that "SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities." Complementing these high-level topics were detailed programming explorations, including articles on memory management in Rust, such as "Box to Save Memory in Rust," and advanced embedded systems work, like "Running Bare-Metal Rust Alongside ESP-IDF on the ESP32-S3's Second Core." For those interested in the physical intersection of code and creation, the hardware category featured practical tutorials, notably the "Clay
